channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: How Black leaders in New York are grappling with Eric Adams and representation
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It wasn’t a shock to many Black New Yorkers that Mayor Eric Adams has surrounded himself with African American civil rights leaders, clergy and grassroots activists since his indictment last week on federal bribery charges.
item.dc:creator: AARON MORRISON and MATT BROWN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The flood of ghost guns is slowing after regulation. It's also being challenged in the Supreme Court
item.description: The Supreme Court is hearing arguments in a case over a federal regulation aimed at reducing weapons often referred to as ghost guns.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Mormon faith pushes ahead with global temple building boom despite cool reception in Las Vegas
item.description: A worldwide building boom of big, bright Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints temples is meeting pushback in a place not known for moderation.
item.dc:creator: Ken Ritter And Hannah Schoenbaum, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump calls new January 6th accusations 'pure election interference'
item.description: The former president spoke exclusively to NewsNation about the unsealing of documents related to his election interference case.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police delivered a 'beatdown' that killed Tyre Nichols, prosecutor says in trial closing
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Memphis police officers who beat Tyre Nichols to death wanted to punish him after he ran from a 2023 traffic stop and thought they could get away with it, a prosecutor said Wednesday during closing arguments in the federal trial of three of the officers.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Seattle judge releases accused killer 27 hours after 65-year-old military veteran stabbed to death
item.description: A judge in Washington state released a 19-year-old man accused of stabbing a 65-year-old handyman and veteran to death at a home in Renton.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Northern border sector sees over 19K illegal immigrants caught in FY 24, more than past 17 years combined
item.description: The 19,222 illegal immigrants caught in fiscal year 2024 was a higher figure than the past 17 years combined, authorities said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police step up patrols around synagogues ahead of Jewish holidays
item.description: While there are no known threats at this time, police are stepping up patrols ahead of the Jewish high holidays.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Burr, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane Helene's victims include first responders who died helping others
item.description: Falling trees and raging floods from Hurricane Helene killed more than 150 people when it struck Florida late last week and then plowed through the Southeast as one of the deadliest storms in U.S. history.
item.dc:creator: BRENDAN FARRINGTON and DAVID FISCHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department finds Georgia is 'deliberately indifferent' to unchecked abuses at its prisons
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia prison officials are “deliberately indifferent” to unchecked deadly violence, widespread drug use, extortion and sexual abuse at state lockups, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: BY JEFF MARTIN, KEVIN McGILL and ALANNA DURKIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Poll: Who won the Walz-Vance VP Debate?
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - Political scientists will tell you that vice presidential nominees rarely swing the election in favor of the candidate at the top of the ticket. But poll after poll shows this election cycle coming down to a percentage point across a handful of key swing states.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, KRON4

item.title: CBS cuts mics of Vance, Walz during immigration argument
item.description: The sparring started when Brennan clarified for debate viewers that Springfield “does have a large number of Haitian migrants who have legal status, temporary protected status.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KRON4

item.title: Trump says he would veto national abortion ban
item.description: Former President Trump on Tuesday said he would veto a federal abortion ban if it made it to his desk in a potential second term after previously refusing to say whether he would do so.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Trump downplays troop injuries in 2020 missile strike: 'You mean because they had a headache?'
item.description: "So first of all, injured. What does injured mean? Injured means — you mean because they had a headache? Because the bombs never hit the fort," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: School board to pay $575K to teacher fired for not using transgender student's pronouns
item.description: A Virginia school board has agreed to pay a former high school teacher more than $500,000 in damages and attorneys' fees as part of a legal settlement after the instructor's dismissal in 2018 for refusing to use a transgender student's pronouns.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WGN 9

item.title: WWII destroyer found off California coast 76 years later
item.description: A surviving ship from WWII that was used by both Allied and Axis forces in the Pacific and that was given a ceremonial burial at sea has finally been located after more than seven decades underwater.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Nobert, KRON4

item.title: McDonald’s is bringing back its famous ‘Boo Buckets,’ with a new color
item.description: McDonald's famous Boo Buckets are making a return just in time for Halloween, and a new color has joined the lineup this year.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KRON4

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs accused of sexual misconduct by 120 people, attorney says
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — An attorney said Tuesday he is representing 120 accusers who have come forward with sexual misconduct allegations against Sean “Diddy” Combs, the hip-hop mogul who is awaiting trial on sex trafficking charges.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A chemical cloud moving around Atlanta's suburbs prompts a new shelter-in-place alert
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Residents east of Atlanta were again warned Tuesday to take shelter where they are if shifting winds push the still-billowing chemical cloud from a chlorine factory fire over their neighborhood.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Workday down? User reports indicate problems with platform
item.description: Is your Workday down? You might not be alone. According to Downdetector.com, reports of people having problems with Workday spiked shortly after 10 a.m. Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, Fox 5

item.title: A judge considers whether to clarify 2 new Georgia election certification rules
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A judge is considering whether to issue clarification or guidance for two new rules from the Georgia State Election Board that have to do with county certification of election results.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ken Page, who starred in 'Cats' on Broadway and voiced Oogie Boogie, dies at 70
item.description: Ken Page, a stage and screen actor who starred alongside Beyoncé in “Dreamgirls,” introduced Broadway audiences to Old Deuteronomy in “Cats” and scared generations of kids as the voice of Oogie Boogie, the villain of the 1993 animated holiday film ”The Nightmare Before Christmas,″ has died.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, the latest in a series of escalating attacks in a yearslong conflict between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies that threatens to push the Middle East closer toward a regionwide war.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, KTLA 5

item.title: My car was submerged in floodwaters. Should I start it?
item.description: Storm surges and flooding from Hurricane Helene have caused widespread devastation across parts of the Southeastern U.S., in some cases leaving homes and cars completely underwater.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KRON4

item.title: Israel says Iran has fired missiles at Israel and it warned residents to shelter in place
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — The Israeli military said Tuesday that Iran has fired missiles at Israel, and air raid sirens sounded across the country as residents were ordered to remain close to bomb shelters. Israel and the United States have warned there would be severe consequences if Iran attacks.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, JULIA FRANKEL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: JD Vance, Tim Walz face off in 2024 VP debate: Key takeaways
item.description: Republican J.D. Vance and Democrat Tim Walz met Tuesday in the lone vice presidential debate of the 2024 election, bringing together undercards who have spent two months going after each other and the opposing nominees who top the major-party tickets.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Favre tries to expand his defamation lawsuit against Mississippi auditor over welfare spending
item.description: Favre is not facing criminal charges, but he is among more than three dozen people or businesses the state is suing to try to recover misspent money through the Temporary Assistance for Needy Families program.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 3 ex-officers did not testify at their trial in Tyre Nichols' death
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Lawyers for three former Memphis officers rested their cases Monday without calling them to testify in their own defense against charges that they violated Tyre Nichols’ civil rights in a beating that proved fatal following a 2023 traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A century of Jimmy Carter: Longest-living former president turns 100
item.description: In 1998, at the ripe age of 74, former President Carter put some of his thoughts about getting older to paper. "The virtues of aging," Carter wrote in a book that year, "include both the blessings that come to us as we grow older and what we have to offer that might be beneficial to others.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: With New York City aqueduct in repair, mayor promises water will remain safe and tasty
item.description: New York City officials assured residents that water flowing from their faucets will continue to be perfectly safe — even if it might taste a bit different — as they announced plans Monday to address massive leaks in the supply system by shutting down part of a main aqueduct for the winter.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Biden administration expands asylum restrictions at southern border
item.description: The Biden administration announced Monday it will expand its asylum restrictions at the U.S.-Mexico border, cementing tougher standards that will cut off access to protections for many migrants.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: It's a 'very difficult time' for U.S. Jews as High Holy Days and Oct. 7 anniversary coincide
item.description: Known as “The Days of Awe,” Judaism’s High Holy Days — which begin on Wednesday — annually provide an emotional mix of celebration, introspection and atonement for Jews around the world.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY and GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about the Walz-Vance debate
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D) and Sen. JD Vance (R-Ohio) are set to go head-to-head on Tuesday night in their first and likely only debate as vice presidential contenders.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5

item.title: A dockworkers strike could shut down Port Tampa Bay. Will it affect holiday shopping?
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. ports from Maine to Texas could shut down if a union representing about 45,000 dockworkers carries through with a threatened strike early Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON and ANNE D'INNOCENZIO AP Business Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: When do hurricane names get retired?
item.description: With nearly two months to go until the end of what was predicted to be a "hyperactive" hurricane season, many residents along Florida's Gulf Coast and southeastern states are grappling with the damage and destruction left in the wake of the latest hurricane.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, ABC 4

item.title: Donald Trump suggests 'one rough hour' of policing will end theft
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — Donald Trump has suggested that “one rough hour” of law enforcement action would tamp down retail theft, an echo of his longstanding support for more aggressive and potentially violent policing.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Stuck NASA astronauts welcome SpaceX capsule that'll bring them home next year
item.description: The two astronauts stuck at the International Space Station since June welcomed their new ride home with Sunday’s arrival of a SpaceX capsule.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
